---
title: "Livestock Marketing Alternatives Explorer"
subtitle: "An open, continuously-updated companion to a forthcoming Arizona Cooperative Extension article, *Market Alternatives and Price Slide Considerations for Selling Feeder Cattle* (Sall & Tronstad, in preparation)."
toc: false
---

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
This site is in **Phase 1 prototype**. One figure (price–weight explorer) is live; more are coming as additional public data pipelines land.
:::

## What this is

A producer-facing, Extension-aligned platform that takes the figures and comparisons in a forthcoming Arizona Cooperative Extension article — *Market Alternatives and Price Slide Considerations for Selling Feeder Cattle* by Sall & Tronstad (in preparation) — and keeps them current as new public USDA and BLS data land. The article itself updates the 1994 *Comparison of Livestock Marketing Alternatives* chapter in the Arizona Ranchers' Management Guide (Tronstad, 1994). Every number on this site is sourced from a public-domain federal dataset; every figure is reproducible from versioned code; every page has an **edit on GitHub** link for corrections.

## Who this is for

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### For producers

A handful of focused, mobile-friendly pages that answer the questions rural producers actually ask: *What did cattle bring last week at weight X?* *Is it better to sell now or retain?* *What does a slide contract cost me in a given market?*

[Start with the price–weight explorer →](price-weight.qmd)
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### For Extension peers & graduate students

Each figure has a **methodology** link explaining what's computed, the deflation choice, and the data vintage. All code is MIT-licensed; every page has an "Edit on GitHub" link so a correction or a state-level adaptation is a pull request away.

[Read the methodology →](methodology/index.qmd)
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### For researchers & journalists

Raw snapshots are committed as Parquet and released annually as a Zenodo deposit with a DOI. Every pipeline run is logged in the `MANIFEST.json`; every figure carries a vintage stamp.

[Browse the data →](data.qmd)
:::

:::

## Featured figure

A quick look at the price–weight explorer, built on archived Clovis, NM feeder-cattle auction data.

```{python}
#| fig-cap: "Preview: feeder cattle price by weight class. Full explorer on the [Price–weight](price-weight.qmd) page."
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 4
#| echo: false

# Placeholder figure. Replaced with a real preview when the Clovis archive
# snapshot lands in data/processed/clovis_archive_2000_2025.parquet.
import plotly.graph_objects as go

fig = go.Figure()
fig.add_annotation(
    text="Preview chart arrives with the Clovis archive snapshot (Phase 1 item #8).",
    x=0.5, y=0.5, xref="paper", yref="paper",
    showarrow=False,
)
fig.update_layout(
    height=300,
    margin=dict(t=20, b=20, l=20, r=20),
    xaxis=dict(visible=False),
    yaxis=dict(visible=False),
)
fig.show()
```

## Last refreshed

```{python}
#| echo: false
import json
from pathlib import Path
# Same pattern as data.qmd — cwd is site/ at render time.
manifest_path = Path("..") / "data" / "processed" / "MANIFEST.json"
if manifest_path.exists():
    with manifest_path.open("r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        manifest = json.load(f)
    entries = manifest.get("entries", [])
    if entries:
        latest = entries[-1]
        print(f"CPI vintage: {latest.get('vintage')} · written {latest.get('written_at_utc')}")
    else:
        print("CPI vintage: not yet populated")
else:
    print("CPI vintage: not yet populated")
```

## Governance

This platform is maintained in the open. The guiding commitment is [**Automation Over Features**](https://github.com/lma-explorer/lma-explorer.github.io/blob/main/GOVERNANCE.md): every new page must be scheduled, must fail loudly, must fall back to last-known-good on failure, must carry a vintage stamp, must have a methodology page, and must be license-compliant — before it ships.

Content: [CC-BY-4.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/). Code: [MIT](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT). Citation: [CITATION.cff](https://github.com/lma-explorer/lma-explorer.github.io/blob/main/CITATION.cff).
